question: The wait-for graph is a deadlock detection algorithm that is applicable when ____________

options.a: all resources have a single instance
options.c: all resources have a single 7 multiple instances

question: An edge from process Pi to Pj in a wait for graph indicates that ____________

options.a: Pi is waiting for Pj to release a resource that Pi needs
options.b: Pj is waiting for Pi to release a resource that Pj needs
options.c: Pi is waiting for Pj to leave the system
options.d: Pj is waiting for Pi to leave the system

question: If the wait for graph contains a cycle ____________

options.a: then a deadlock does not exist
options.c: then the system is in a safe state
options.d: either deadlock exists or system is in a safe state

question: If deadlocks occur frequently, the detection algorithm must be invoked ________
What is the disadvantage of invoking the detection algorithm for every request?

options.a: overhead of the detection algorithm due to consumption of memory
options.b: excessive time consumed in the request to be allocated memory

question: A deadlock eventually cripples system throughput and will cause the CPU utilization to ______
Every time a request for allocation cannot be granted immediately, the detection algorithm is invoked. This will help identify ____________

options.a: the set of processes that have been deadlocked
options.b: the set of processes in the deadlock queue
options.c: the specific process that caused the deadlock

question: A computer system has 6 tape drives, with ‘n’ processes competing for them. Each process may need 3 tape drives. The maximum value of ‘n’ for which the system is guaranteed to be deadlock free is?
A system has 3 processes sharing 4 resources. If each process needs a maximum of 2 units then, deadlock ____________

‘m’ processes share ‘n’ resources of the same type. The maximum need of each process doesn’t exceed ‘n’ and the sum of all their maximum needs is always less than m+n. In this setup, deadlock ____________
